alternate_names: Bundesamt fuer Sicherheit in der Informationstechnik
notes: Germany's federal cyber security authority. BSI publishes compliance criteria catalogues for cloud computing and AI systems, used as de facto regulatory requirements for German government procurement and critical infrastructure.

match_nodes.description: AI Cloud Computing Compliance Criteria Catalogue

data.official_name: AI Cloud Computing Compliance Criteria Catalogue
data.description: BSI criteria for AI systems in cloud environments. Structured as general conditions and specific criteria.
data.notes: BSI criteria for auditing the security and trustworthiness of AI-based cloud services. Covers data management, model development, deployment, and operation of AI systems. Designed to complement the C5 catalogue for cloud-specific AI requirements.

data.description: BSI criteria catalogue for cloud service providers. General conditions and criteria for cloud security.
data.notes: Defines minimum security requirements for cloud computing. Used as the basis for cloud security attestation in Germany. Covers organization, physical security, identity management, cryptography, operations security, and more. C5:2020 is the current version. Required for German federal government cloud procurement.
